title: Use a Dataclass
prompt_md: Define a `@dataclass` named `User` with fields `name: str` and `age: int`. Then write `user_label(name, age)` that builds a `User` and returns the string `"{name} ({age})"`, e.g. `user_label("Aniq", 30)` -> `"Aniq (30)"`.
starter_code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

# define User here

def user_label(name, age):
    pass
hints: Decorate the class: `@dataclass` on the line above `class User:`.
Declare fields with type hints: `name: str` and `age: int`.
Build it with `u = User(name, age)`, then return an f-string `f"{u.name} ({u.age})"`.
